svg.text: This diagram shows how Ralph drives the Claude Code CLI against a target repo while you're AFK.
Each iteration renders prompt templates, then runs implementer → reviewer stages inside an ephemeral Docker sandbox.
The loop exits early when the gate stage returns <promise>NO MORE TASKS</promise>.
Shims (afk.sh / ghafk.sh) → bins (apps/cli, no build) → @daonhan/ralph-core runAfk / runGhAfk → runBin
Prompt assembly before every stage (renderTemplate in render.ts) — expanded in this order
issues.json for ghafk); only the path enters.
Host shell via execSync. !? returns fallback on
failure; plain ! throws and aborts the iteration.

runStage / streamDocker: write .run prompt → docker run --rm -i → parse stream-json NDJSON · ensureImage: inspect → pull → build fallback
Node 22 · .NET SDK 10 · gh · jq · git · Claude Code CLI.
non-interactive approval; final result event = stage output.
plus the Docker API for Testcontainers
when the host socket is mounted.
Same-shell rule: PowerShell and WSL $HOME are
separate stores — log in from the shell you run from.
the loop's entire blast radius. Git env vars injected

Ephemeral --rm container · blast radius = the workspace bind-mount · bypassPermissions confined to it ·
mounted docker.sock is root-equivalent host access — opt out with RALPH_DOCKER_SOCK=0.
(cross-cutting — how Ralph itself ships; release-please automates npm + image versions)
Each iteration loops Gate → Implement → Review with freshly rendered prompts and fresh containers —
the sentinel <promise>NO MORE TASKS</promise> exits early, otherwise the iteration budget runs out.
